q: Sound is a sequence of ___ waves which propagates through compressible media.
A relationship between ___ and pressure determines the speed of sound within the medium.
The ___ of the medium determines the rate at which sound is attenuated.
Speed of a sound wave can also be affected by the density, temperature, and ___ of the medium.
In a solid medium, the speed of sound is given by $v = \sqrt{\frac{Y}{\rho}}$, where $Y$ is ___.
Longitudinal sound waves travel ___ in solids than they do in liquids.
Young's modulus and the bulk modulus are ___ properties of the medium.
The minimum intensity of sound that can be heard is known as the ___ of hearing.
The decibel ($dB$) is the unit used for measuring ___ intensity.
The intensity level of sound is defined as $\beta (dB) = 10 \log_{10} ___$.
For every $10 dB$ added to the sound level, the loudness of the sound is increased by ___ times.
Sound intensity is a direct measure of the ___ reaching an eardrum measured in $W \cdot m^{-2}$.
Echoes are produced where a sound wave is ___ from a large and hard surface.
An ___ is repetition of sound due to the reflection of sound.
Reflection of sound obeys the laws of ___.
Padding and carpets ___ the incident sound and reduce the amount of sound reflecting off surfaces.
Echolocation is the special ability of emitting sounds and interpreting the ___.
A ___ is a reflection of light or sound waves, or a wide reaching impact of an action.
___ means able to be heard or capability of detecting sounds in certain range of frequencies.
The range of frequencies a person can hear is known as the range of ___.
For humans with normal hearing, the lower limit is $20 Hz$ and the upper limit is about ___ $Hz$.
The technology that works based on echoes and is used by ships for navigation is called ___.
Sonar stands for sound navigation and ___.
Infrasound waves ($f < 20 Hz$) have ___ wavelengths and are thus able to travel over long distances.
Infrasound waves travel longer distances with less ___.
The Doppler effect is the change in ___ of a wave related to an observer due to relative motion.
Christian Doppler described the Doppler effect phenomenon in ___.
In the Doppler Equation, $f_o = f_s (\frac{v \pm v_o}{v \mp v_s})$, $f_o$ represents frequency heard by the ___.
The phenomenon called ___ occurs when light from a star observed from earth shifts due to the earth and star moving away from each other.
___ is a form of echolocation that uses electromagnetic waves instead of sound.
Radar stands for radio detection and ___.
Doppler ultrasound is a technique used to examine ___ flow.
The measurement of Doppler shift in frequency of ultrasound reflected by red blood cells is known as ___.
The ___ of the note (how high it is) is determined by its frequency.
The loudness of the note depends upon the ___ of the wave that produces it.
Greater amplitude leads to a louder note because more ___ is used to produce a larger amplitude.
___ or timbre is the characteristic which distinguishes two sounds of the same loudness and same pitch but emitted by different instruments.
The timbre of a sound depends on its ___, which varies with the number of overtones.
___ is a sensation and hence, unlike intensity, it depends on the observer.
The intensity of a wave is directly proportional to the ___ of the amplitude of the wave.
